title: Mastering Academic Vocabulary: English & Spanish for Grades 6-8
description: A comprehensive vocabulary pack designed for newcomer ELL students in grades 6-8, focusing on essential academic terms for classroom success in English and Spanish. This pack includes 20 key vocabulary words, a mini-book for contextual learning, and three engaging worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A student breaking down a complex problem into smaller parts with a magnifying glass
Different puzzle pieces coming together to form a complete picture
A person weighing two options on a scale, making a judgment
A large book being condensed into a small notebook with a few key points
Two apples side-by-side, highlighting their similarities
A black cat and a white cat standing next to each other, showing differences
A detective looking at clues to figure out a mystery
A simple sketch being expanded with many detailed drawings and notes
A student presenting reasons and evidence to support an answer
A word in the center of a circle, surrounded by other related words and images
Footprints on the ground leading to a lost item, showing proof
A thought bubble with a question mark and a possible explanation
A complex diagram with interconnected ideas, representing a well-explained concept
Two people debating respectfully, each presenting their case
Two people looking at the same object (e.g., a '6' or '9'), seeing different numbers
Sorting different colored blocks into separate piles
A person looking at a piece of art and explaining its meaning
A teacher drawing a picture on a whiteboard to explain a concept
A finish line with a runner crossing it, representing the end of a process or a final decision
A person showing step-by-step how to build a model

worksheets.instructions_en: Match each English academic vocabulary word to its correct English definition. Draw a line from the word to its definition.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Une cada palabra de vocabulario académico en inglés con su definición correcta en inglés. Dibuja una línea de la palabra a su definición.

items.content_l2: Analizar
options: To give reasons or evidence to support an answer or claim.
To break down information into smaller parts to understand it better.
To form an opinion or judgment about something.
To combine different ideas or pieces of information to create something new.
To state the main points of something in a brief form.
To find similarities between two or more things.
To find differences between two or more things.
To explain with more details.
To figure out something based on evidence and reasoning, not directly stated.
The circumstances or information surrounding an event or word that help to understand it.
items.correct_answer: To break down information into smaller parts to understand it better.

worksheets.instructions_en:
Read each sentence. Fill in the blank with the correct academic vocabulary word from the word bank.
Word Bank: Evidence, Hypothesis, Theory, Argument, Perspective, Differentiate, Interpret, Illustrate, Conclude, Demonstrate
worksheets.instructions_l2:
Lee cada oración. Completa el espacio en blanco con la palabra de vocabulario académico correcta del banco de palabras.
Banco de palabras: Evidencia, Hipótesis, Teoría, Argumento, Perspectiva, Diferenciar, Interpretar, Ilustrar, Concluir, Demostrar

items.content: The scientist formed a ______ before starting the experiment.
items.content_l2: El científico formuló una ______ antes de comenzar el experimento.

items.content: We need strong ______ to support our claims in the essay.
items.content_l2: Necesitamos ______ sólida para respaldar nuestras afirmaciones en el ensayo.

items.content: Can you ______ between a fact and an opinion?
items.content_l2: ¿Puedes ______ entre un hecho y una opinión?

items.content: The teacher asked us to ______ the meaning of the poem.
items.content_l2: La maestra nos pidió ______ el significado del poema.

items.content: Please ______ your answer with a diagram or example.
items.content_l2: Por favor, ______ tu respuesta con un diagrama o ejemplo.

items.content: After reviewing all the data, we can ______ that the experiment was successful.
items.content_l2: Después de revisar todos los datos, podemos ______ que el experimento fue exitoso.

items.content: Each student presented their ______ on the topic during the debate.
items.content_l2: Cada estudiante presentó su ______ sobre el tema durante el debate.

items.content: His ______ was well-organized and included three supporting reasons.
items.content_l2: Su ______ estaba bien organizado e incluía tres razones de apoyo.

items.content: Evolution is a scientific ______ supported by decades of research.
items.content_l2: La evolución es una ______ científica respaldada por décadas de investigación.

items.content: The student was able to ______ her understanding by solving the problem on the board.
items.content_l2: La estudiante pudo ______ su comprensión resolviendo el problema en la pizarrón.

worksheets.instructions_en: Sort the following academic vocabulary words into the correct category. Write each word under the heading where it best fits.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Clasifica las siguientes palabras de vocabulario académico en la categoría correcta. Escribe cada palabra debajo del encabezado donde mejor corresponda.

items.content: Words to sort: Analyze, Summarize, Elaborate, Justify, Infer, Evaluate, Synthesize, Compare, Contrast, Interpret, Illustrate, Demonstrate, Conclude, Differentiate, Context, Evidence, Hypothesis, Theory, Argument, Perspective

items.correct_answer: Analyze, Infer, Evaluate, Synthesize, Compare, Contrast, Differentiate, Interpret
Summarize, Elaborate, Justify, Illustrate, Demonstrate, Argue, Conclude

mini_book.title: Our Big Project: Using Academic Words

pages.text_en: Page 1: Our teacher, Ms. Elena, gave us a big project. We need to understand how to use new academic words to do well.
pages.text_l2: Página 1: Nuestra maestra, la Sra. Elena, nos dio un gran proyecto. Necesitamos entender cómo usar nuevas palabras académicas para hacerlo bien.

pages.text_en: Page 2: First, we need to ANALYZE the topic. This means breaking it down into smaller parts to understand each piece.
pages.text_l2: Página 2: Primero, necesitamos ANALIZAR el tema. Esto significa dividirlo en partes más pequeñas para entender cada pieza.

pages.text_en: Page 3: Next, we will COMPARE and CONTRAST different ideas. We'll look for similarities and differences between sources.
pages.text_l2: Página 3: Luego, COMPARAREMOS y CONTRASTAREMOS diferentes ideas. Buscaremos similitudes y diferencias entre las fuentes.

pages.text_en: Page 4: We must find strong EVIDENCE to support our ARGUMENT. Our claims need proof!
pages.text_l2: Página 4: Debemos encontrar EVIDENCIA sólida para apoyar nuestro ARGUMENTO. ¡Nuestras afirmaciones necesitan pruebas!

pages.text_en: Page 5: It's important to consider every PERSPECTIVE. Everyone has a different point of view, and we need to understand them.
pages.text_l2: Página 5: Es importante considerar cada PERSPECTIVA. Todos tienen un punto de vista diferente, y necesitamos entenderlos.

pages.text_en: Page 6: After gathering information, we will SYNTHESIZE it. This means combining all the different parts into one clear idea.
pages.text_l2: Página 6: Después de recopilar información, la SINTETIZAREMOS. Esto significa combinar todas las partes diferentes en una idea clara.

pages.text_en: Page 7: Finally, we will EVALUATE our work. We will judge its quality and make sure it meets all the requirements.
pages.text_l2: Página 7: Finalmente, EVALUAREMOS nuestro trabajo. Juzgaremos su calidad y nos aseguraremos de que cumpla con todos los requisitos.

pages.text_en: Page 8: Using these academic words helps us clearly DEMONSTRATE our learning and CONCLUDE our project successfully!
pages.text_l2: Página 8: Usar estas palabras académicas nos ayuda a DEMOSTRAR claramente nuestro aprendizaje y CONCLUIR nuestro proyecto con éxito.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to understand, identify, and begin to use academic vocabulary words essential for success in middle school content areas. Students will develop strategies for comprehending and applying these terms in English and Spanish.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Pre-teaching: Introduce words before a unit or lesson. During Reading: Encourage students to identify and infer meaning of words in context. Post-Reading: Use words in summaries, discussions, and writing assignments. Writing Activities: Provide sentence frames and graphic organizers for students to practice using the vocabulary. Pair Work: Students can practice definitions and create sentences together.
materials_needed: Optional: Bilingual dictionaries, digital devices for image search
differentiation_tips: For beginners: Provide sentence starters for all writing tasks. Allow students to use L1 for initial understanding before transitioning to English. Use gestures and realia whenever possible.
For intermediate learners: Encourage creation of concept maps or Frayer models for each word. Have them write short paragraphs using multiple vocabulary words.
For advanced learners: Challenge them to use the words in debates or presentations. Ask them to explain the nuances between similar words (e.g., 'hypothesis' vs. 'theory').
Visual support: Always pair words with images or physical actions.
Peer support: Encourage collaborative learning where students can explain words to each other in L1 or L2.

answers.10: The circumstances or information surrounding an event or word that help to understand it.
